speaker: MATT
raw_text: The lights swish. All right. So, to get you guys up to speed as fast as I can-- this is a really long arc. The Briarwoods, being a family that five years ago took over the city of Whitestone, where Percy hails from, murdered his family, and he barely escaped with his life, years later, came back into the folds of Vox Machina's existence, and it was decided to go ahead and hunt them down and save the city of Whitestone. Upon arriving, they began to stir rebellion in the town against this horrible undead menace controlled by Lady Briarwood herself. After this rebellion was incited, fires were burning, individuals were killed that were responsible for keeping the people down and just generally bad people all around were being slaughtered by our intrepid adventurers. They made their way into Whitestone Castle, where they had uncovered some information about the ziggurat, a project in which the Briarwoods had been developing something beneath the city of Whitestone, and discovered that Percy's sister Cassandra, who was thought to be dead long ago, actually still survived and was being kept in the castle. She joined the party as you guys fought your way down underneath the city of Whitestone through a series of tunnels, finding yourselves unintentionally locked within a trap, a retooled version of what was previously an acid distillery for the whitestone that's actually mined from the surrounding mountains, and thought left for dead by the Briarwoods themselves. In which Vax was charmed and taken along with them, and Cassandra showed herself--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yep. We'll leave it at that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You can't do any better that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So, tomorrow you'll be able to read this really inaccurate description by Sam Riegel-- the Critter's Guide to Critmas. And last but not least, if anybody has any other announcements or projects coming up that they'd like to mention. There's a Wii U game that just came out called Xenoblade Chronicles X where I voice a character named Lao, that just came out recently, so if you're a fan of those awesome RPGs, I will be really subdued and arrogant in your face.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Subdued and arrogant.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We get that for free.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Exactly. Every week. Anything else, guys? We good?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty. Now let's get down to the nitty gritty. (sound effect noises)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Was that "Who Wants to be a Millionaire?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Of course. It's got to be, right?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You haven't taken a short rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I shoot a holy arrow at it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Then let's roll some initiative. (yelling)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We're fighting the thing that's been in your head!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Therapy!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: There's so many allegories!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Therapy!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We're fighting the demons in your head.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) That's pretty great, actually.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: My therapist would be so proud right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh man, we're out of like everything, aren't we?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, we're great, we're fine, we're good.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We got a guy with a hammer. We're fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh jeez, okay. Well, I've still got two 3rd-level spells.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I need stats on Lord Briarwood's sword.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right, take your d10. This moment of intensity, as you turn around now at this point, seeing everyone striking, you've looked your head over and you can see for the first time in full physical form, the creature that has haunted your dreams for the past five years. While it is not present, you can feel it smile. It's your turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What? What did you, what was that?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Lightning.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Either or magic. One of the two didn't affect it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The lightning bolt passes through it and it strikes the back of the wall and you can see the electrical energy discharge across the stone and earth, arcing and spidering off until it dissipates, seeming to not have any sort of impact or effect on the billowing smoke entity.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You want to stay there or you want to move? You are in its melee range. You are in its melee range, so if you move it will get an attack of opportunity if it chooses to take it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (stammers) I will not move and instead I will inspire Percy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: And I'll say, you feel good in this motherfucker! Your whole hood in this motherfucker! (laughs) That's it. (laughter)

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right. It is now Vax's turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, take your d10. This moment of intensity, as you turn around now at this point, seeing everyone striking, you've looked your head over and you can see for the first time in full physical form, the creature that has haunted your dreams for the past five years. While it is not present, you can feel it smile. It's your turn.

preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: And cut open her bonds.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so you pull out the-- okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Take out my sword and cut open her bonds. Get back! Run! And then, if I still have enough timing beyond that, I'd love to take a swipe at it with my sword.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Unfortunately, it's going to, to free her entirely of her bonds and give her that, it's going to be your action.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay. We haven't had a short rest, have we?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We're not looking good.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: 22? Okay. In the brief moment you got to see it, the way it moves, the way it completely cast aside Scanlan's attack, and the way that it housed itself within Percy for so long. There are elements of it that make you think of a shadow demon. But usually, they're not this large. They're not this powerful. Either this is just a powerful entity of a similar type, or it's been feeding over time, or it's something beyond that, but you get the sense that this thing is demonic in nature, based on the brief appearance you had with it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I hand her my sword.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. She takes the rapier and looks at it and goes running away, back towards where Castle Whitestone is.

preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.text: Can I do something?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If you want to, instead of holding your action, cast something, you can do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'm going to go up to Percy and cast Protection from Evil on him.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Whoa!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Which...

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What are you going to choose? You get to choose a certain type of creature which he's protected against.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Do we know anything about Orthax?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You have no idea. You saw it briefly. I'm going to have you roll a religion check. Being a religiously trained individual, let's see if you can ascertain the nature of this creature, this entity.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Yep, so that would be times two. Just confirming. It has been a while since we've had any sort of spell-based crit. So that would be 42 points of radiant damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Oh my god. I rolled a one and a natural 20. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. So at 4th level, that would be 7d6 radiant damage.

preceding_turns.text: Whoa!

preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: For the purpose of ease, I usually double. I'm just confirming one thing real fast.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, so go ahead and do your math.

preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Please?

preceding_turns.text: Come on, come on.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Eight. All right. You can see now, it turns to you for a second and the Mark of Vengeance fades as suddenly you feel like you've damaged it mentally enough to the point where it doesn't even want to deal with you anymore. You're far too mean.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (laughing) You're too mean.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're going to stay where you are?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, that ends your turn. Percy, you're up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys all glance over. The shackles that are on the ground are now collapsed and empty.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: With the gag safely removed from her mouth during this allowed her to cast Misty Step, which is a verbal-based spell. She is currently--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: How far away?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I'm calculating that right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: And can I see her?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: She doesn't look back at you. She just keeps walking. You follow her for about five minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: She's naked. Good job, brother.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, she's mostly nude, other than her undergarments.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I toss the robe back on her.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Vax, Cassandra, let's take her to the acid pits.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The only thing she has left is a ring on her finger.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What about those bracers you were talking about?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Cassandra, remembering this, drags her body through what remnant is there on the ground. There's a big groove left behind her as she's being dragged through it. You get to the second trap that she marked, and you guys make your way around it. You make your way to where the road diverges, the one way to the castle, and the one to the acid pits. And Cassandra heads towards the acid pits.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Cassandra, what are you doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: She's throwing them in the acid pits.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: After about five minutes of following her, the path leads up and then crests, and there is a room that smells awful. It's similar to the acrid scent the room was filled with when the acid was pouring into the room. There's a whole series of other chemicals and inside, you can see there's a number of laboratory set-ups near you. You get the feeling this is probably where a lot of this particular acidic concoction that was fine-tuned for this stone of Whitestone was created, or at least, honed by Ripley. Without a beat, Cassandra walks up to the edge and just throws Delilah's body in. (plop, sizzle) Then it disappears beneath the surface as the hissing, bubbling noise takes over the silence of the room. She sits there and just watches it sink.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The only thing she has left is a ring on her finger.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What about those bracers you were talking about?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Cassandra, remembering this, drags her body through what remnant is there on the ground. There's a big groove left behind her as she's being dragged through it. You get to the second trap that she marked, and you guys make your way around it. You make your way to where the road diverges, the one way to the castle, and the one to the acid pits. And Cassandra heads towards the acid pits.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Cassandra, what are you doing?

preceding_turns.text: She's throwing them in the acid pits.

preceding_turns.text: She doesn't look back at you. She just keeps walking. You follow her for about five minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Are we sure this won't make anything bad happen if we throw a body into the acid pits?

preceding_turns.text: I think it's fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I think it's acid.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: A few moments pass. You don't even see it beneath the surface now. You just see a little bit of some fume released from whatever slow process is dissolving your gun.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes. I go run and jump on Grog's shoulders.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you watch it arc forward, in your head, you're like, "That fucking gnome." The spell fades, you hear him say that, and just as it (plop, sizzle).

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You're not going to get that, are you?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I reach in to try and grab it out.

preceding_turns.text: No, Percy! And I reach out and I grab his hands.

preceding_turns.text: He threw it! You threw it, though, right?

preceding_turns.text: No, he dropped it!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He lobbed it. It wasn't a huge throw.

preceding_turns.text: On?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Percy!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: As I reach up and I grab his hands as well!

preceding_turns.text: That was so expensive!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a wisdom saving throw with advantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Son of a fuck.

preceding_turns.text: Heroes' Feast, workin' against you!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Wisdom saving throw?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What's your DC?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: My difficulty class? 18.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're like, "That cost a-- (cracking)"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) I always fail rolls against Percy! It sucks!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay, your muscle control comes back to you and Cassandra is holding your freshly slapped face, gives you a look and goes, "Brother. Let's go to our people. Let's tell them that for the first time in five years Whitestone is free."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You fucking genius!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA

preceding_turns.text: It was so expensive.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Cassandra steps up to you and takes your head in her hands and forces you to look at her and goes, "Brother. We've all made sacrifices for this." She slaps you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (yelps)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh! That's a 21. 19 plus two.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: He's held right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Still holding him in place.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I hold my fist way up high to Scanlan on Grog's shoulder for a silent little exploding fist bump.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And she looks to you and goes-- Cassandra gives you a look to--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You were never Mother's favorite.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's not be petty.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: She told me!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's not be petty, Percy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You sure you want to let him go? You sure? Did you hear what he just said to you? You sure?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It came out as (muffled angry sounds).

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I let it go.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Front door? All right, Grog walks up to the front door, being careful of the scattered caltrops you guys left there. The door is still ajar from where you left it. As you step out and look into the daylight, and it's at this point now, probably mid-afternoon, pushing into evening. You guys have been traveling underground for quite some time and taken a few short rests, and have had a lot of conflict and things happen. So you started at the start of the day. You're now pushing into towards dusk. But as you open up and look outside, with what daylight is still there, you see little plumes of smoke coming from the far city. Down the pathway that leads up to Castle Whitestone, you see about 70 to 100 armored humanoids marching up towards the front of the castle.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: But you're still alive!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm not alive!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You're not controlled anymore! [break]

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Welcome back, folks! So. Let's go ahead and pick up where we left off. So, as you guys return to the undercroft of Whitestone, Cassandra carrying you, arm in arm. You make your way up through the stairs to the main base floor of Castle Whitestone. Are you guys heading towards your-- where are you heading from here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to head straight for the front door and look in-- is there a balcony on top?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can check for the second floor if you want to.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Overviews the city?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You have a pretty high view. It's already elevated on this platform, so even from a ground-floor area you can look down into the city.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Front door.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Pushing further in you can see now the clouds beginning to dissipate, and also for the first time since you've been here you see blue sky directly above the city. The vibrant orange-red-purple colors of the sunset now come cascading along the city of Whitestone, and there's a collective (gasps), an inhale and gasp of awe from the soldiers and the people alike as this opens up above you. You begin to walk past. The Alcove's to your left, if you wish to--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Pike is right here.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Your husband died for a good cause, thanks, bye. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Pike is shocked. Pike is shocked and offended.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Why are you looking at me like that, Pike? Pike, stop it!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you walk away, she looks really confused and clutches her chest in horror for a moment as one of the soldiers walking with you steps by her side, looking at you curiously, and gives her a kiss on the cheek. She goes, "Who was that man?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Can we-- definitely want to go, let's stop by the Alcove.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You eventually start making your way towards the Alcove as you enter deeper into the city.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Pike, you there? Can you hear us?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Have I cleared up enough of the sky at this point?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: We're going to get to that right now, actually.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I want to get to that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you guys are stepping-- I'm sorry, what?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, it's okay.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As the sun slowly sets, the light coming through in beautiful colors now. You see what was once a very bleak town suddenly be renewed with a spectrum of new accents. You begin to hear music being played, as now the celebration begins with the sun's setting. Folks are turning this into a full celebration now. People are starting to bring out food and--

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: (mic bump)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Pardon me." (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Damn it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Start that one again?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's still very beautiful.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So beautiful.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Two years on a sitcom. I can hold this all day. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: God damn it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Percival's just waiting for his introduction.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: To the stone-cold motherfucker on air.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "To the return of those valiants we once thought lost, and those who rise up to restore the joy of these people. Percival. May we raise our weapons, our glasses, and our children, who accept this future to rebuild. Raise to Vox Machina. Hyah!" And everyone raises as one loud--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah! Vox Machina!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "All right, sir." And the boy takes the gold and rushes off, looking in his hands at the boon he's been given by this practically-same-height man. They don't see gnomes here very often; it's been very confusing.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: They've just had this shit in a Tupperware container, like in those big bins in the back for the last five years with like duct-tape lids.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's not a grand meal, there hasn't been a lot of time to prepare for it, but basically what it looks like is whoever is preparing dinner or whatever food was readily available, they're just bringing it out. It's more of like a share-with-whoever's-there type effort.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Aw, it's a potluck.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Kind of.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I find a young boy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, Scanlan?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Or a soldier, and I give him 15 gold and I say, for the love of god, find me a fucking beret.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: A few moments pass before the boy returns, in his hand a (singing) raspberry beret, the kind you find in a secondhand store.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Aw, it's a potluck.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Kind of.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I find a young boy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, Scanlan?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Or a soldier, and I give him 15 gold and I say, for the love of god, find me a fucking beret.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "All right, sir." And the boy takes the gold and rushes off, looking in his hands at the boon he's been given by this practically-same-height man. They don't see gnomes here very often; it's been very confusing.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I want to take some time to tell Keeper Yennen about what is beneath the tree and let him start to macerate some theories on what to do--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Macerate? That's a big word.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: After you finish this conversation, he sits there and--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's a word for big name. Thank you. Do it.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Yes. Keyleth, at this point, as you've walked away from this conversation, you approach the Sun Tree. It stands there with a little bit of light as the sun's just now passing over the distant mountain range that surrounds this valley. The sky's growing more purple and dark blue. But now lights have been lit and lanterns have been brought to the center, you can see the Sun Tree in full before you.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: If most of their books have been taken from them, perhaps we could use some of the treasury to build a library.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That is not a terrible idea, and it's possible that the books are still within the castle.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's possible that the Briarwoods have hoarded all of them. In fact, we might find something about this entity inside the castle.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: And we do need to build three new structures where the lords' houses were, and I find the idea of rebuilding those mansions to be a bit distasteful.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Perhaps we could even name one of the libraries Gilmore's Glorious Books.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Set up a satellite chain?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We will discuss it. It's got a ring, I will admit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: While you guys are having this conversation--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I was just going to add to that, I'm sure the man can help in this city's time of need.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You were doing something, as well.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. As this is happening and he walks off, you guys are there at the table, just now noticing the both of them have been missing. You see Keyleth, stunned, standing by the Sun Tree as Keeper Yennen approaches you once again and goes, "My friends. We have a lot of work to do and it would not be fair for me to assume, as you've already done so much for us, but it has been a while since we've had much to celebrate in this town, and this evening seems a fine improvisational event. But we have two weeks until the Winter's Crest festival would be happening here in Whitestone, something we have not celebrated in near five years. I would just ask, should you be interested, if you would wish to stay with us these next couple of weeks to help us rebuild and perhaps aid in preparing the first real shining moment in Whitestone's history since you were gone."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I didn't say it out loud, I just thought it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, you told her.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh yeah, no, you said that out loud. Yeah, no, that wasn't in your head.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I wore a mask for a long time, where I grew up. And I'm not going to do that anymore. I'll wait.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Wait?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: For you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I don't know what else to say right now. I'm really tired. Really tired. I think there's also a part of me that's just afraid.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I feel like I'm going to get pantsed by a goliath at any moment. Don't do anything. Don't change anything. I haven't lied. I've told the truth. If you'll have me, I'm yours. If you don't want me, I understand. And that's all there is to say. And then I actually walk off.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I stay by the tree.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: And so as the rest of you join in the merriment, or whatever else you wish to do for the evening, looking about post-sunset as night descends on Whitestone, to the first clear sky since you've arrived. To the first evening where you can hear smiles, cheers, and laughter, we'll pick up next week with the Winter's Crest festival in Whitestone. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Percival?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I would be very grateful.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We're here.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We shall.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Cassandra nods, as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Keeper, may I ask for a small request? Could you maybe send a wonderfully worded letter to Uriel and let him know how we saved the day and how much you love us?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He looks very confused. "I imagine that could be-- I assume that could be done, yes."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That would be wonderful. Make sure you mention how terrible the Briarwoods were and how Vox Machina saved the day. Truly saved it--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We can write it for you if you just want to sign it at the bottom.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That guy's going to wake up this morning and go, "oh, I'm an asshole."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Please? Yes, that would be wonderful.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "All right. It'll be done."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Facepalm.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: "Aw, what was I thinking. Oh."
